title: Interdisciplinary STEAM Challenge
description: Test your knowledge across a variety of scientific and technological fields, from 3D printing to astronomy! This 10-question challenge pulls from different topics to broaden your STEAM horizons. Good luck!

questions.questionText: Which of the following is a common technology used in 3D printing to create objects layer by layer?
questions.explanation: FDM is one of the most widely used 3D printing processes, extruding thermoplastic filament to form layers.

questions.questionText: Which property of a sound wave determines its pitch?
questions.explanation: The pitch of a sound is primarily determined by its frequency (measured in hertz, Hz).

questions.questionText: Which force primarily counteracts gravity to keep an airplane aloft during flight?
questions.explanation: Lift, generated by airflow over the wings, acts perpendicular to the relative wind and opposes the weight of the airplane.

questions.questionText: What is the slope of a line given by the equation y = 3x + 5?
questions.explanation: In the slope-intercept form y = mx + b, m represents the slope. Here, m = 3.

questions.questionText: Which technique separates mixtures based on differences in their movement through a stationary phase?
questions.explanation: Chromatography separates components based on differential affinities between the mobile phase and the stationary phase.

questions.questionText: Which organ is primarily responsible for filtering and removing waste products from the blood?
questions.explanation: The kidneys filter waste from the bloodstream and excrete it as urine.

questions.questionText: Which branch of AI focuses on learning from data without being explicitly programmed?
questions.explanation: Machine Learning allows systems to learn from patterns in data rather than following strictly programmed instructions.

questions.questionText: Which celestial objects are formed from the gravitational collapse of massive stars and have a gravitational field so strong that nothing can escape?
questions.explanation: Black holes form when extremely massive stars collapse and create a point of infinitely dense matter.

questions.questionText: Which macromolecule is primarily responsible for catalyzing biochemical reactions in living organisms?
questions.explanation: Enzymes are proteins that speed up biochemical reactions by lowering the activation energy.

questions.questionText: Which biotechnology technique involves using living cells or enzymes to synthesize products like antibiotics or hormones?
questions.explanation: Fermentation uses microorganisms or enzymes to convert substrates into valuable products, such as antibiotics and hormones.

rewards.description: Earn 700 points for completing this interdisciplinary challenge!
